vllm.title: [TPU] Fix greedy decoding
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR fixes the greedy decoding implementation in the TPU backend.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix PaliGemma MMP
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The linear layer in PaliGemma MMP was previously initialized as a ColumnParallelLinear. This was not intended as we haven't supported TP-sharded MMP. This PR fixes it by changing it to nn.Linear like all other VLMs.
FIXES #6910

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix use of per-request seed with pipeline parallel
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The current per-request seed implementation assumes that the sampling happens in the same process as the driver worker since the sampler SequenceGroup objects are used to hang the torch.Generators on to maintain state between steps.
This assumption is now not always true and in particular this is wh

vllm.title: [Kernel] Remove scaled_fp8_quant kernel padding footgun
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
@LucasWilkinson and I were considering using the batch_dim_padding to see if this helps the illegal memory access we're currently debugging (see e.g. #6767). However I realized that it's not compatible with the cutlass kernels.
This PR fixes that issue by padding the scale vector whenever it pads th

vllm.title: [core][misc] improve free_finished_seq_groups
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
free_finished_seq_groups is in the critical path, because the engine calls it after every model step.
the main branch searchs for all the requests to find finished requests, while new finished requests should only appear in running queue.
this simple optimization, turns out to be important, when we

vllm.title: [Build] Temporarily Disable Kernels and LoRA tests
vllm.author: simon-mo
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This is mostly a cost saving measures at the moment as we are running low on CI cost. Developers on these should still run test locally for confirmation, if their code touches relevant code path.

vllm.title: [Nightly benchmarking suite] Remove pkill python from run benchmark suite
vllm.author: cadedaniel
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The comment says sometimes kill does not work, so instead we do pkill on python. This doesn't quite right -- kill -9 [gpu proc pid] should be equivalent in signalling power to pkill python.
This allows me to run the benchmark locally while I have other python processes open, e.g. infrastructure moni

vllm.title: [CI] [nightly benchmark] Do not re-download sharegpt dataset if exists
vllm.author: cadedaniel
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This saves disk space when running benchmark repeatedly.

vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The follow_imports option has been enabled for the following files and directories:

vllm/*.py (originally failed mypy, made passing in this PR)
vllm/adapter_commons (originally failed mypy, made passing in this PR)
vllm/assets
vllm/inputs
vllm/logging
vllm/multimodal
vllm/platforms
vllm/server
vllm

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix broadcasting logic for `multi_modal_kwargs`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently multi_modal_kwargs are placed on GPU before distributed broadcast. Since the broadcast operation only allows flat dictionaries of tensors, custom logic is required to handle nested tensors. Currently, it only handles singly-nested dictionaries and fails to consider nested lists of tensors.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Fix mypy errors
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: There have been some changes during the development of #6681 that caused additional mypy errors which fail CI. This PR fixes them.
